title: celmisia glandulosa var. latifolia
intro: Celmisia glandulosa var. latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved mountain daisy, is a flowering plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The broad-leaved mountain daisy is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, with its leaves and flowers being used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
